artifacts.summary:
## Overview
The meeting discussed key review metrics, including MR rates, defect tracking, and security work prioritization, with the goal of improving productivity, quality, and security. The team agreed to track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time and to hold the narrow MR rate at 10 as the target going forward.

## Key Discussion Points
### MR Rates
* The team discussed the difference between RD Overall and RD Wider MR rates, with the latter including community contributions and the former not.
* The team agreed to simplify the metrics by tracking the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
* The narrow MR rate was discussed, with Christopher noting that it was significantly below target and hoping it would bounce back.
* The team discussed the impact of seasonal dips and changes in team productivity on the narrow MR rate.

### Defect Tracking
* The team discussed defect tracking and the measurement of average open box age.
* The team agreed to measure the percentage of open bugs that are within SLOs, rather than just closed bugs.
* The team discussed the need to dig deeper into the data to understand the spike in mean time to close.

### Security Work Prioritization
* The team discussed the need to get more security work prioritized, as it is not currently reflected in the security metrics.

### SUS Scores
* The team discussed SUS scores, with Craig noting that the decline in Q4 was the smallest in several quarters.
* The team agreed to keep an eye on SUS scores, as they cannot assume that the current trend is a real upward direction.

### Bug Discussion
* The team discussed the underlying problem in metrics measurement and the need to address it.

### Rate Discussion
* The team discussed the narrow MR rate and agreed to hold it at 10 as the target going forward.

## Decisions Made
* The team will track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
* The narrow MR rate will be held at 10 as the target going forward.
* The team will focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.

## Open Questions
* The team needs to be cautious not to assume a trend in SUS scores without enough data.
* The narrow MR rate may be affected by seasonal dips and changes in team productivity.
* The team needs to dig deeper into the data to understand the spike in mean time to close.
artifacts.followups:
## Email
Subject: Recap of Engineering Key Review Meeting

Dear All,

This email serves as a recap of the Engineering Key Review meeting held on February 18, 2021. The meeting aimed to discuss key review metrics, including MR rates, defect tracking, and security work prioritization. The main decisions and action items from the meeting are as follows:

* The team will track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time, replacing the current complex metric.
* The narrow MR rate will be held at 10 as the target going forward.
* The team will focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.

Action items:

* Lily will work with Max to implement the new metric.
* Steve will partner with Mac to address the database replication issue.
* Ryan will lead the effort to improve defect tracking and measure average open bug age.
* Sid will review and adjust the security metrics to reflect prioritized security work.

Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns regarding these decisions and action items.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

## Slack
"Recap of Engineering Key Review: We've decided to track the percentage of total MRs from the community over time, replacing the current metric. The narrow MR rate will be held at 10 as the target going forward. Action items: Lily & Max to implement the new metric, Steve & Mac to address database replication, Ryan to improve defect tracking, and Sid to adjust security metrics."

DECISION CUES:
- The team will track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
- The narrow MR rate will be held at 10 as the target going forward.
- The team will focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.

CAUTIONS:
- The discussion around MR rates and defect tracking highlighted underlying problems in the metrics measurement.
- The team needs to be cautious not to assume a trend in SUS (System Usability Scale) without enough data.

COORDINATION BRIEF

PARTICIPANTS:
- Eric Johnson
- Lily
- Sid
- Max
- Christopher
- Mike
- Rob
- Ryan
- Craig
- Steve
- Mac
- Sus

SCOPE:
The meeting discussed key review metrics, including MR rates, defect tracking, and security work prioritization. The goal is to improve productivity, quality, and security.

DECISION CUES:
- The team will track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
- The narrow MR rate will be held at 10 as the target going forward.
- The team will focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.

CAUTIONS:
- The discussion around MR rates and defect tracking highlighted underlying problems in the metrics measurement.
- The team needs to be cautious not to assume a trend in SUS (System Usability Scale) without enough data.
- The narrow MR rate may be affected by seasonal dips and changes in team productivity.

## Overview
The meeting discussed key review metrics, including MR rates, defect tracking, and security work prioritization, with the goal of improving productivity, quality, and security. The team agreed to track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time and to hold the narrow MR rate at 10 as the target going forward.

## Key Discussion Points

### MR Rates and Defect Tracking
* The team discussed the difference between RD Overall and RD Wider MR rates, with RD Wider including both community contributions and internal MRs.
* The team agreed to simplify the metrics by tracking the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
* The team discussed the issue of MR rates not moving because it's a productivity rate, and agreed to focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.

### Security Work Prioritization
* The team discussed the need to get more security work prioritized, but noted that this is not currently reflected in the security metrics.
* The team agreed to work on making sure security work prioritization is reflected in the metrics.

### SUS (System Usability Scale)
* The team discussed the decline in SUS scores, but noted that it's still an improvement from previous quarters.
* The team agreed to keep an eye on SUS scores and not assume a trend without enough data.

### Defect Tracking
* The team discussed the issue of defects meeting SLOS, with S1 and S2 at 80% and 60% respectively.
* The team agreed to focus on measuring the percentage of open bugs that are within SLOS.

### Narrow MR Rate
* The team discussed the narrow MR rate being below target, but noted that it's higher than last year.
* The team agreed to hold the narrow MR rate at 10 as the target going forward.

## Decisions Made

* Track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
* Hold the narrow MR rate at 10 as the target going forward.
* Focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.

## Open Questions

* How to address the underlying problem in metrics measurement.
* How to prioritize security work and reflect it in the metrics.
* How to improve SUS scores and not assume a trend without enough data.
* How to measure the percentage of open bugs that are within SLOS.
* How to address the issue of defects meeting SLOS.

## Email
Subject: Key Review Meeting Recap

Dear All,

This email serves as a recap of the key review meeting held on February 18, 2021. The meeting focused on reviewing key metrics, including MR rates, defect tracking, and security work prioritization.

Key decisions and action items from the meeting include:

* Tracking the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
* Holding the narrow MR rate at 10 as the target going forward.
* Focusing on other indicators to improve quality and security.
* Simplifying the MR rate metrics to prevent gaming.
* Working on a new KPI to measure the percentage of total MRs that come from the community.
* Improving defect tracking and security work prioritization.
* Reviewing and adjusting the security metrics to reflect the team's efforts.

Action items:

* Lily and Max will work on transitioning to the new KPI.
* Steve will partner with the data team to improve the database and address the replication lag.
* Rob will continue to improve defect tracking and security work prioritization.
* Craig will review and adjust the security metrics.

Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

## Slack
"Key review meeting recap: We'll track the percentage of total MRs from the community, hold the narrow MR rate at 10, and focus on other indicators for quality and security. Action items: Lily and Max on the new KPI, Steve on database improvements, Rob on defect tracking, and Craig on security metrics. Let me know if you have any questions or concerns."
